definitions: A specific (method + URL) on your server that does one particular thing, that the frontend calls to send or get data
Written as: HTTP Method + URL path (e.g. POST /check-url)
A 'door' on your server that receives or sends data
note: Frontend calls the backend, which has an API. That API branches into different endpoints — each endpoint handles one specific task (e.g. /check-url, /submit-feedback, /get-stats).

definitions: Everything that runs on the server — behind the scenes

Backend = API + Authentication + Background Jobs + Business Logic + Caching + Configuration + Database + Error Handling + External API Integration + File Storage + Logging + Middleware + Security + Server Logic

types.name: Data
types.description: Storing, fetching, updating, deleting data in databases

types.name: Authentication & Authorization
types.description: Login systems, checking permissions, who can access what

types.name: Business Logic
types.description: All the rules and calculations — pricing, discounts, algorithms, validation

types.name: Processing/Computing
types.description: Heavy tasks like image resizing, video compression, complex calculations

types.name: External Services
types.description: Connecting to payment processors, email services, SMS, other APIs

types.name: Real-time Communication
types.description: Routing messages between users, live updates, notifications

types.name: Security
types.description: Encryption, spam detection, rate limiting, protecting sensitive operations

types.name: Background Jobs
types.description: Scheduled tasks, automated processes, cleanup operations

definitions: Putting your project on the internet so others can access it
Renting space on someone else's server to run your code — makes your website/backend available 24/7
Before hosting: your project only works on your computer. After hosting: anyone worldwide can use it.

types.name: Backend Hosting
types.description: For your Node.js server — Render, Railway

types.name: Frontend Hosting
types.description: For your landing page — Vercel, Netlify

definitions: Code that runs between the request and the response
Checks requests from the frontend for security issues like invalid data, unauthorized access, and spam attacks
Only safe requests are forwarded to the backend — malicious ones are blocked before they cause harm
A set of rules that define how something should be done
Route = endpoint + the code that handles it
When frontend calls an API endpoint, the backend code/function that handles that endpoint is called a route
note: Think of it like a button (endpoint) and the logic behind it (route). You press the button → the route defines what happens.

definitions: A computer/program that listens for and responds to requests — the 'thing' that's always running and waiting to serve
Backend is the code/logic that runs on the server

types.name: Examples of servers
types.description: A physical computer in a data center (AWS, Google Cloud), your laptop running Node.js during development, Render/Railway hosting service, Apache/Nginx (web server software)

types.name: Examples of backend
types.description: Your Node.js + Express code files, API endpoints, routes and handler functions, database connection code, middleware, authentication code

note: Server = computer/program (the thing running). Backend = the code on it (what it does).

definitions: Programs and instructions that tell a computer what to do — the non-physical part of a computer
Uniform Resource Locator — the address/location of something on the internet
note: URL path: the part after the domain name that specifies which resource you want. Example: in 'example.com/check-url', the path is '/check-url'.
